evidence_excerpt: After subtotal resection of the jugular foramen chondrosarcoma and later gross-total resection of the suprasellar pituitary adenoma, the patient had significant improvements; at 2-year follow-up, voice hoarseness and visual acuity were significantly improved, indicating recovery with residual symptoms.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: At 2-year follow-up, residual voice hoarseness and visual acuity issues were present but significantly improved.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: After subtotal resection of the left jugular foramen lesion, there was significant improvement of dysphagia and auditory acuity.
Postoperative improvement in dysphagia indicates improved swallowing function.
Ten months after the first operation, the patient underwent gross-total resection of the suprasellar lesion (pituitary adenoma).
At the 2-year follow-up, the patient’s voice hoarseness and visual acuity were significantly improved.

notes_on_uncertainty: Exact dates are not provided; the index time was approximated to the diagnosis of Maffucci syndrome with associated intracranial lesions prior to surgical treatment. Long-term functional status is inferred from symptom descriptions, as formal functional assessments are not reported.
